meta_title: Impact Windows in Lauderdale Manors, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact windows in Lauderdale Manors, FL. ESW & CWI brands, 170mph wind protection, financing from $87/mo. Call today!
h1: Impact Windows in Lauderdale Manors, FL — Serious Storm Protection for Real South Florida Living
intro:
Lauderdale Manors is a tight-knit, working-class community tucked inside Broward County's urban core, flanked by Fort Lauderdale to the east, Oakland Park to the north, and Lauderhill to the west. Life here revolves around hard-earned homes, neighborhood pride, and the kind of community spirit that keeps streets alive around Lauderdale Manors Park. But what every homeowner on these blocks also shares — whether they talk about it or not — is the ever-present reality of South Florida's hurricane season. Sitting at just four miles from the Atlantic coastline, Lauderdale Manors sits squarely inside Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), the most demanding wind-hazard classification in the entire state of Florida. That designation means the wind speeds, debris loads, and storm surge threats here demand nothing less than the highest-rated building products available — and that conversation starts with impact windows.

With an average home value around $260,000, homeowners in Lauderdale Manors are making serious investments in their properties, and protecting those investments makes both financial and practical sense. The community is composed largely of mid-century concrete block and frame homes, with an estimated 65% of structures built before 1994 — meaning most were constructed before Florida's modern hurricane building codes took effect following the catastrophic lessons of Hurricane Andrew. Many of those older single-hung and jalousie windows are still in place today, offering little more than a pane of glass between your family and a Category 3 storm. Upgrading to certified impact-resistant windows isn't just a smart home improvement; in Lauderdale Manors, it's one of the most responsible decisions a homeowner can make.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've helped hundreds of Broward County homeowners make exactly that transition. Known throughout South Florida as 'The Kings of Service,' our team brings 60+ years of combined installation experience to every project, with 100% in-house crews who know the unique construction styles, permit processes, and structural quirks of neighborhoods like Lauderdale Manors inside and out. Whether you're replacing single windows or doing a full whole-home upgrade, we handle every step — from the initial in-home consultation through final inspection with the Broward County Building Division — so you never have to chase a subcontractor or wonder about the quality of work being done in your home.

Our 60+ years of combined experience isn't a marketing number; it's what allows our team to navigate the specific framing styles, masonry details, and window openings common in the older homes throughout the 33311 ZIP code with precision and skill.

We also understand that affordability matters deeply in a community like Lauderdale Manors. That's why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down, making it genuinely possible for homeowners at every income level to access the storm protection they deserve. You get peace of mind from start to finish — and that's exactly what being 'The Kings of Service' means to us.

section_history.h2: Lauderdale Manors Has Faced the Storm — Here's What That History Teaches Us
section_history.body:
Broward County has been tested by at least 10 significant tropical systems since 1990, and Lauderdale Manors has felt the effects of every major one. The story really begins with Hurricane Andrew in August 1992, which made landfall in Miami-Dade but sent devastating winds and debris far northward through Broward County. The structural damage revealed a brutal truth: the building standards of the era were woefully inadequate, and South Florida's housing stock — much of it still standing in communities like Lauderdale Manors today — was dangerously vulnerable to even sustained tropical-force winds. Andrew forced Florida to completely rethink and rebuild its building codes, but that reform came too late for the thousands of homes already standing.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 struck as one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded, making a direct hit across Broward County and leaving widespread damage from West Broward all the way through Fort Lauderdale and its surrounding neighborhoods. Lauderdale Manors homeowners experienced roof damage, downed trees, and broken windows — the latter being responsible for much of the interior water intrusion and structural compromise that followed. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which barreled up Florida's peninsula and reminded the entire state that storm risk never really goes away. Wind gusts well above tropical storm force swept through this part of Broward County, once again exposing the vulnerability of older single-pane windows and hollow-core doors.

The pattern across all three storms is consistent: homes without impact-rated windows and doors suffered dramatically more damage — not just from direct wind pressure, but from the cascading effect of breached openings that allowed wind to pressurize interiors, lift roofs, and flood living spaces. For homeowners in Lauderdale Manors, especially those living in pre-1994 construction, the lesson is clear. The question is no longer whether another storm will test your home — it's whether your home will be ready when it does.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Broward County Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows
section_code.body:
Lauderdale Manors sits within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), a designation that encompasses Miami-Dade and Broward Counties and represents the strictest residential building standard in the United States. The HVHZ classification was established by the Florida Building Code specifically because of the catastrophic storm exposure these counties face, and it mandates that any impact window product installed in the area must pass a rigorous series of tests including large missile impact testing, cyclic wind pressure testing, and water infiltration resistance standards. Not every impact window on the market qualifies — only products that have earned Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval (FPA) under HVHZ protocols may be legally installed in your home.

For Lauderdale Manors specifically, the Florida Building Code requires that all fenestration (windows and doors) must be rated to withstand design wind speeds of at least 170 mph in the 33311 ZIP code. This is not a suggestion — it is a code requirement enforced by the Broward County Building Division, which oversees all residential permit activity in unincorporated and incorporated Broward communities. Any window replacement project in Lauderdale Manors requires a permit to be pulled with the Broward County Building Division prior to installation, followed by a mandatory field inspection before the permit can be closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every element of this process on your behalf, ensuring that your project is fully documented, legally compliant, and eligible for all associated insurance and resale benefits.

It's also worth noting that homes built before 1994 in Lauderdale Manors may require additional framing or structural reinforcement around window openings to bring the installation into full code compliance. Our experienced in-house team identifies these needs during the initial consultation and addresses them as part of the project scope — never as a surprise add-on. Doing the job right means doing it by the book, and in Broward County's HVHZ, that standard is non-negotiable.

section_products.h2: ESW Eastern Storm Windows and CWI Impact Products Built for Broward County's HVHZ
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premier product lines specifically selected for their performance in South Florida's most demanding wind and weather conditions: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI impact windows. Both brands are engineered and tested to meet HVHZ standards, and both carry the Miami-Dade and Florida Product Approvals required for legal installation in Lauderdale Manors. When you're shopping for impact windows in the 33311 area, the brand on the frame matters — and these are among the most trusted names in the South Florida market.

ESW Eastern Storm Windows are manufactured with multi-chamber extruded aluminum or vinyl frames engineered to resist flex and deformation under sustained 170 mph wind loads. The laminated glass used in ESW products consists of two panes of tempered or annealed glass bonded by a durable PVB (polyvinyl butyral) interlayer that holds the glass intact even after impact from wind-borne debris. This means that even in a worst-case scenario, your window doesn't shatter outward — it cracks and holds, maintaining the weather barrier that protects your home's interior. ESW windows are also available with Low-E coatings that significantly reduce solar heat gain, a feature that directly translates to lower cooling costs during Broward County's long, brutal summers.

CWI impact windows offer an equally impressive performance profile, with product lines suited to everything from standard single-hung residential windows to custom-sized picture windows and architectural specialty shapes common in older Lauderdale Manors homes. CWI frames feature thermally broken profiles that reduce heat transfer through the frame itself, complementing the energy efficiency of Low-E glass. Together, ESW and CWI give Premier Impact Windows & Roofing the flexibility to match the right product to your home's specific window sizes, aesthetic preferences, and performance needs — while always staying within the HVHZ compliance requirements that Broward County demands.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Windows Cost in Lauderdale Manors — And How Do You Finance Them?
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions homeowners in Lauderdale Manors ask is straightforward: what is this going to cost me? The honest answer is that it depends on the number of windows being replaced, the sizes and configurations involved, and whether any structural prep work is needed around older window openings. For a typical home in the 33311 ZIP code — a 3-bedroom, 2-bath concrete block house in the $240,000–$280,000 range — a full-home impact window replacement using ESW or CWI products generally ranges from $8,000 to $18,000, depending on scope. That number may feel significant, but when measured against the value it adds to your home, the insurance savings it generates, and the storm damage costs it prevents, the return on investment becomes very compelling very quickly.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options specifically designed to make that investment accessible for homeowners across all income levels. With financing starting at just $87 per month and $0 down payment options available for qualified applicants, you can begin protecting your home immediately without depleting your savings. Our team works with you to find a financing structure that fits your monthly budget, and we're transparent about terms from the very first conversation. For a community like Lauderdale Manors where many residents are balancing home improvement priorities carefully, this flexibility makes a real difference.

Beyond the upfront economics, consider what impact windows do for your home's long-term value. In a neighborhood where the average home is valued around $260,000, adding HVHZ-certified impact windows is one of the highest-ROI improvements you can make. Studies consistently show that impact window upgrades recover 70–85% of their cost in appraised home value, while simultaneously generating annual insurance premium savings that can reduce the effective cost of the investment over time. When you factor in energy savings from Low-E coatings — which can cut cooling costs by 15–25% annually in South Florida's climate — the windows begin paying for themselves well before another hurricane season arrives.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Lauderdale Manors Homeowners with Impact Windows
section_insurance.body:
If you own a home in Lauderdale Manors and you're paying for homeowner's insurance — and with a mortgage, you almost certainly are — then you're already aware of how dramatically Florida insurance premiums have climbed in recent years. Broward County homeowners are among the hardest hit in the state, with wind insurance and Citizens Property Insurance premiums increasing significantly as carriers grapple with storm risk and reinsurance costs. The good news is that installing certified impact windows is one of the most powerful tools available to reduce those premiums, often generating annual savings that make the investment even more financially attractive.

Under Florida law, insurance carriers are required to offer premium discounts to homeowners who have completed a wind mitigation inspection and can demonstrate that their home has qualifying opening protection. Impact windows certified to HVHZ standards — like the ESW and CWI products installed by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — qualify for the highest tier of opening protection credit, which is the single largest driver of wind insurance discounts available to residential policyholders. Depending on your carrier, your current premium, and the age and construction of your home, these discounts can range from 15% to 45% off your annual wind insurance cost. For homeowners in the 33311 ZIP code currently paying $3,000–$5,000 or more annually for wind coverage, that translates to hundreds of dollars in savings every year.

After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your impact window installation and your project receives its final inspection from the Broward County Building Division, we recommend scheduling a licensed wind mitigation inspector to document your home's new level of protection. That report goes directly to your insurance carrier and triggers the discount review process. Many of our Lauderdale Manors customers find that their annual insurance savings alone cover a meaningful portion of their monthly financing payment — effectively making the windows more affordable than they appeared at first glance. It's one of the few home improvements that genuinely works on multiple financial fronts simultaneously.

faqs.q: Do impact windows really make a difference for homes in Lauderdale Manors that aren't right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While Lauderdale Manors sits about four miles from the Atlantic coastline, it remains firmly inside Broward County's High Velocity Hurricane Zone. Wind speeds during a major hurricane don't diminish meaningfully over four miles of urban terrain. Flying debris — roof tiles, fence posts, tree limbs — becomes a lethal projectile threat long before storm surge ever reaches your neighborhood. HVHZ-rated impact windows protect against both wind pressure and debris impact, making them just as essential for inland Lauderdale Manors homes as for beachfront properties.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost in Lauderdale Manors and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a typical Lauderdale Manors home, a full impact window replacement with ESW or CWI products generally ranges from $8,000 to $18,000 depending on the number of windows, sizes, and any needed structural prep. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified applicants. We work with you to find a payment plan that fits your budget so you can start protecting your home without delay. Insurance savings and energy cost reductions help offset the investment over time.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace windows in Lauderdale Manors, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes — all impact window replacements in Lauderdale Manors require a permit from the Broward County Building Division, followed by a mandatory field inspection before the permit can be closed. Broward County's HVHZ requirements mean the permitting process also verifies that the products being installed carry the appropriate Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance or Florida Product Approval. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permit process on your behalf, from application through final inspection, so you never have to navigate the bureaucracy yourself.

faqs.q: How long does a full impact window installation take for a home in Lauderdale Manors?
faqs.a: For most Lauderdale Manors homes — typically 8 to 15 windows — our 100% in-house installation crew completes the physical installation in one to two days. The overall project timeline from signed contract to final permit closure generally runs three to six weeks, with the majority of that time allocated to permit processing and inspection scheduling with the Broward County Building Division. We keep you informed at every stage and work to minimize disruption to your household throughout the process.

faqs.q: Will impact windows really lower my homeowner's insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes, and often significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation discounts to homeowners with qualifying opening protection, and HVHZ-certified impact windows — like the ESW and CWI products we install — earn the highest available credit tier. Broward County homeowners frequently see annual wind insurance reductions of 15% to 45% after a licensed wind mitigation inspection documents the upgrade. For homeowners currently paying $3,000–$5,000 or more annually in wind coverage, those savings can amount to hundreds of dollars per year, making impact windows one of the most financially strategic home improvements available.
